title: Terminal Mental Models, Help, and Evidence · Incident Repair Bay
summary: Investigate an unfamiliar Linux workstation without guessing, changing host state, or treating copied commands as explanations. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.
Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.
Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — On a debug host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 0. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.

Inspect the Shell Terminal Kernel trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.

content.text: For Shell Terminal Kernel, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Shell Terminal Kernel precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: On a debug host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 0. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: The terminal window is the Linux shell and executes commands itself.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Shell Terminal Kernel, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe Linux operations investigation headed "# Evidence: linux-terminal-evidence-record-1jj34w" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Terminal Evidence Record into observable performance evidence.

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Terminal Evidence Record: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — On a debug host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 0. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
